steps.title: Calculate the volume of the sphere
steps.content:
Use the sphere volume formula with $r = 3$ inches. Substituting:

$$V = \frac{4}{3}\pi (3)^3 = \frac{4}{3}\pi (27)$$

Simplify: $\frac{4 \times 27}{3} = \frac{108}{3} = 36$, so

$$V = 36\pi \text{ cubic inches}$$

steps.title: Multiply volume by density to find mass
steps.content:
Mass = Density $\times$ Volume:

$$M = 51 \times 36\pi = 1836\pi \text{ ounces}$$

That matches choice D — D is correct.
